title: polygonum aviculare subsp. calcatum
intro: Polygonum aviculare subsp. calcatum, also known as knotweed, is a subspecies of Polygonum aviculare, a type of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Polygonum aviculare subsp. calcatum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant effects. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with herbal preparations often using the leaves and stems of the plant.
